---
title: "Output Pipeline Metadata"
format: html
---

`OutputPipeline` defines the reusable metadata used to build and validate
DeepLearning output chains. It keeps the internal distinction between
tensor-to-tensor transforms and terminal tensor-to-DataManager decoders, while
allowing the UI and JSON model specs to present a single ordered pipeline.

The compatibility helpers propagate tensor shape metadata without running
inference. Spatial outputs such as `[C,H,W]` may terminate in spatial decoders
or pass through transforms such as global average pooling. Vector outputs such
as `[C]` may terminate in `TensorToFeatureVector`.

Each `OutputPipelineStepSpec` stores the step id plus an optional typed
parameter variant. Transform settings such as `SpatialPointModuleParams` and
decoder settings such as `MaskDecoderParams` are therefore owned by their
concrete step type rather than flattened into one catch-all struct.

The default pipeline helpers currently encode the main built-in workflows:
NeuroSAM probability maps default to `TensorToMask2D`, while General Encoder
feature maps default to `global_avg_pool -> TensorToFeatureVector`.
